commands.review_summary:
The PR makes `runAgentHarnessAgentEndHook` awaitable, awaits it in local CLI terminal paths, keeps Codex app-server callers fire-and-forget, and updates hook docs plus focused tests.

Reproducibility: yes. from source inspection, but not from an executed current-main repro in this read-only review. Current `main` drops the `agent_end` promise in local CLI terminal paths, so `runPreparedCliAgent()` can resolve or throw before async hook work settles.
commands.review_followup: No automated repair is needed; the merge blocker is contributor real-behavior proof plus maintainer acceptance of the bounded CLI exit-latency change.

target.title: fix(agent): await agent_end hooks for local CLI runs
target.body:
## Problem

Short-lived `openclaw agent --local` runs can finish the CLI action before `agent_end`
observation hooks have completed. That is fine for persistent Gateway/app-server
runtimes, but it is unsafe for one-shot CLI runs where trusted plugins use
`agent_end` to flush terminal state such as observability spans, memory capture, or
session-end bookkeeping.

In the observed local OTel failure, `before_agent_start` and `agent_end` both fired
for `openclaw agent --local`, but the process could return without waiting for the
async `agent_end` work that should close and flush the terminal `openclaw.agent.turn`
span.

## Change and value

This PR makes `runAgentHarnessAgentEndHook` awaitable and has the local CLI runner
await it at every terminal `agent_end` call site. The helper still catches and logs
hook failures, and the hook runner's existing timeout remains the guardrail for slow
or wedged plugins.

Persistent Codex app-server paths keep their prior non-blocking behavior by calling
the now-async helper with `void`, and the plugin hook docs now describe that split:
Gateway/persistent paths remain fire-and-forget, while short-lived one-shot CLI paths
wait before cleanup.

## Who is affected, and who is not

- Affected: operators and trusted plugins relying on `agent_end` during
  `openclaw agent --local`, especially terminal observability or capture plugins.
- Affected: local CLI runs may wait up to the configured `agent_end` timeout when a
  trusted plugin is slow.
- Not affected: Gateway and persistent Codex app-server runs continue to fire
  `agent_end` without blocking the main runtime path.
- Not affected: hook failures still log and continue rather than failing the agent
  turn.

## Why now

This fixes a concrete observability reliability hole in `openclaw agent --local`:
the lifecycle hook fired, but the short-lived process did not provide a deterministic
drain point for async terminal hook work. The change is small, scoped to the CLI
harness terminal paths, and covered by a regression test that proves the CLI result
does not resolve until `agent_end` settles.

## Implementation

- Make `runAgentHarnessAgentEndHook` return `Promise<void>` and await the registered
  hook runner internally.
- Await that helper in every `runPreparedCliAgent` terminal path: blocked
  `before_agent_run`, success, retry success, retry failure, failover failure, and
  generic failure.
- Mark Codex app-server calls as explicit `void` fire-and-forget calls to preserve
  persistent runtime behavior.
- Update plugin hook docs to describe the persistent-runtime versus one-shot-CLI
  behavior split.

## Real behavior proof

**Behavior or issue addressed**

`openclaw agent --local` could return from the local CLI runner before async
`agent_end` hook work completed, so terminal plugin flush/capture work was not a
reliable part of the one-shot run.

**Real environment tested**

- Source worktree: `/tmp/openclaw-pr-agent-local-otel`
- Branch/commit: `fix/agent-local-agent-end-hooks` at `bed0a8d2d8`
- Base: `origin/main` at `229323d37a`
- Installed local runtime sanity check: OpenClaw `2026.5.19 (a185ca2)` on Node
  `v26.1.0`

**Exact steps or command run after this patch**

```bash
node scripts/run-vitest.mjs run --config test/vitest/vitest.unit.config.ts \
  src/agents/harness/lifecycle-hook-helpers.test.ts \
  src/agents/cli-runner.reliability.test.ts \
  --maxWorkers=1

pnpm exec oxfmt --check --threads=1 \
  docs/plugins/hooks.md \
  extensions/codex/src/app-server/run-attempt.ts \
  src/agents/cli-runner.reliability.test.ts \
  src/agents/cli-runner.ts \
  src/agents/harness/lifecycle-hook-helpers.test.ts \
  src/agents/harness/lifecycle-hook-helpers.ts

git -C /tmp/openclaw-pr-agent-local-otel diff --check
```

Local generated-dist before/after probe against the installed helper:

```bash
node --input-type=module -e 'import { t as runAgentHarnessAgentEndHook } from "/home/captain/.nvm/versions/node/v26.1.0/lib/node_modules/openclaw/dist/lifecycle-hook-helpers-B3NLL-Hy.js"; /* probe calls helper with a manually released agent_end promise */'
```

**Evidence after fix**

- Focused Vitest files: pass.
- Formatting check over the six touched files: pass.
- `git diff --check`: pass.
- Installed generated-dist probe:

| State | `isPromise` | `hookCompletedBeforeRelease` | `resultResolvedBeforeRelease` | `hookCompletedAfterRelease` | `resultResolvedAfterRelease` |
| --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- |
| Before local dist patch | `false` | `false` | `false` | `true` | `false` |
| After local dist patch | `true` | `false` | `false` | `true` | `true` |

**Observed result after fix**

The helper now returns a promise and that promise remains unresolved until the
`agent_end` hook promise is released. The new CLI regression test verifies
`runPreparedCliAgent()` remains unresolved while `agent_end` is pending and resolves
only after the hook settles.

**What was not tested**

- I did not claim full end-to-end OTel JSONL proof with a live external model
  provider. A sandboxed `openclaw agent --local --model ollama-cloud/glm-5.1`
  baseline hit DNS failure, and rerunning it outside the sandbox would send local
  agent context to an external provider. That needs explicit operator approval or a
  fully isolated mock-provider harness.
- Full CI-shaped build/type/lint matrix was not run locally on the WSL host.

## Risks

One-shot CLI runs with slow trusted `agent_end` plugins can now take longer to exit,
up to the existing hook timeout. That is intentional for terminal capture/flush
correctness and bounded by the hook runner.
target.author: kaspre
